title: AMD Q1 2023 Earnings Call Summary
text: AMD delivered Q1 2023 revenue of $5.4 billion, down 9% year-over-year. The company reported diluted earnings per share of $0.60, exceeding expectations despite mixed market conditions. Data Center revenue remained flat year-over-year at $1.3 billion. While EPYC CPU sales to cloud customers grew double-digits, enterprise demand declined due to macroeconomic uncertainty. The company launched 28 new AMD-powered instances, bringing the total to over 640. Client revenue plummeted 65% to $739 million as AMD intentionally shipped below consumption to reduce downstream inventory. Management stated: 'we believe the first quarter was the bottom for our client processor business.' Gaming revenue declined 6% to $1.8 billion, with semi-custom revenue growth offset by lower graphics sales. Embedded revenue surged to a record $1.6 billion, significantly up year-over-year, driven by Xilinx acquisition integration and strength across industrial, automotive, and communications markets. AMD reorganized its AI efforts under Victor Peng, consolidating teams to accelerate hardware and software development. Management emphasized: 'AI is our No. 1 strategic priority,' highlighting the MI300 GPU launch planned for later in 2023. For Q2 2023, AMD expects approximately $5.3 billion in revenue (flat sequentially), with gross margins around 50%. Management projects double-digit data center growth for the full year, supported by Genoa and Bergamo processor ramps.
